title: calophanes littoralis
intro: Calophanes littoralis, also known as sea calophane, is a type of lichenized fungus that grows on rocks and trees in coastal areas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of certain compounds like usnic acid. The health benefits of Calophanes littoralis include its potential to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, Calophanes littoralis is often used in the form of tinctures or infusions, and is typically combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
